name: vercel
context: Vercel deployment platform â€” instant deploys, serverless functions, edge middleware, domains, and environment variables
info: The Vercel ecosystem covers the full deployment lifecycle for modern web applications. Vercel provides instant Git-based deploys with automatic preview URLs, serverless functions for backend logic (Node.js, Python, Go, Ruby), edge middleware for low-latency compute at CDN nodes, custom domain management with automatic SSL, and scoped environment variables for development, preview, and production. This tree is organized around the jobs an agent actually performs: deploying projects, creating serverless functions, configuring edge middleware, managing domains, and setting environment variables. Next.js is the first-class framework, but Vercel supports any frontend framework.
body:
# Vercel Ecosystem

Vercel is a deployment platform for modern web applications. It connects to Git repositories and produces instant preview deployments on every push, with zero-config builds for Next.js, Nuxt, SvelteKit, Astro, and other frameworks. Serverless functions handle backend logic, edge middleware runs at CDN nodes for low-latency request processing, and custom domains get automatic SSL.

## Agent Jobs

The primary tasks an agent performs in this ecosystem:

- **Deploy projects** â€” push to Git or use the CLI to trigger builds, create preview deployments, and promote to production. See [[vercel/deploy]].
- **Create serverless functions** â€” write API routes and handlers that run on-demand in Node.js, Python, Go, or Ruby runtimes. See [[vercel/serverless]].
- **Configure edge middleware** â€” build low-latency middleware and edge functions that run at CDN nodes worldwide. See [[vercel/edge]].
- **Manage domains** â€” add custom domains, configure DNS records, and provision SSL certificates. See [[vercel/domains]].
- **Set environment variables** â€” define secrets and configuration scoped to development, preview, or production. See [[vercel/env-vars]].

## Frameworks

Vercel provides zero-config support for popular frameworks:

| Framework | Build Command | Output Directory |
|-----------|---------------|------------------|
| Next.js | `next build` | `.next` |
| Nuxt | `nuxt build` | `.output` |
| SvelteKit | `svelte-kit build` | `.svelte-kit` |
| Astro | `astro build` | `dist` |
| Remix | `remix build` | `build` |
| Vite (React/Vue) | `vite build` | `dist` |

## CLI

- **vercel** â€” deploy from the command line
- **vercel dev** â€” run the development server locally with serverless function support
- **vercel env** â€” manage environment variables
- **vercel domains** â€” manage custom domains
- **vercel secrets** â€” manage encrypted secrets (legacy, prefer env vars)

sections.title: Platform overview
sections.body: Vercel builds and deploys frontend applications from Git. Every push creates an immutable deployment with a unique URL. Preview deployments are created for branches and pull requests; production deployments are triggered by merges to the production branch. Builds run in isolated containers with configurable Node.js versions. Output is served from a global CDN with automatic edge caching.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.body: CLI authentication uses `vercel login` for interactive flows or the `VERCEL_TOKEN` environment variable for CI/CD and agent workflows. API requests use Bearer token auth: `Authorization: Bearer $VERCEL_TOKEN`. Org-scoped operations also require `VERCEL_ORG_ID`. Project-scoped operations require `VERCEL_PROJECT_ID`. Generate tokens at https://vercel.com/account/tokens.

sections.title: Git integration
sections.body: Vercel integrates with GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket. Connecting a repository enables automatic deployments on push. Preview deployments are created for pull requests with status checks and comment previews. The production branch (usually `main`) triggers production deployments. Configure branch protection and ignored build steps in project settings.
